title: quercus galeanensis
intro: Quercus galeanensis, also known as the Mexican white oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to Mexico and parts of Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high tannin content, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus galeanensis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues, wounds, and infections. Quercus galeanensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat conditions such as diarrhea, fever, and sore throats.
